skill.name: Skywork Search
skill.description: Search the web for real-time information using the Skywork web search API. Use this skill whenever the user needs up-to-date information from the internet - for example, researching a topic, looking up recent events, finding facts or statistics, gathering material for a document or presentation, or answering questions that require current data.
skill.summary: Real-time web search via Skywork API with OAuth authentication and multi-query support.
skill.author: SkyworkAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 48 patterns but most are false positives. Subprocess calls open browser URLs for OAuth (hardcoded, no injection risk). Token storage in ~/.skywork_token follows standard CLI patterns. All network calls go to legitimate Skywork API endpoints. No malicious intent detected - skill performs documented web search functionality.

low_findings.title: Subprocess for Browser Launch
low_findings.description: Uses subprocess.Popen to open browser URLs for OAuth authentication. URLs are constructed from hardcoded constants and API responses, not user input. Low risk as no injection vector exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code analysis confirms subprocess calls use only hardcoded OS commands with trusted URL strings from API responses.

low_findings.title: Local Token Storage
low_findings.description: Stores OAuth token in ~/.skywork_token file. Standard pattern for CLI authentication but tokens are stored unencrypted. User should ensure proper file permissions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token file path and write operation confirmed in code - standard but unencrypted credential storage.

low_findings.title: External API Dependencies
low_findings.description: Makes network requests to Skywork API endpoints (api.skywork.ai, api-tools.skywork.ai). Required for skill functionality but creates external dependency and potential data exposure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded API endpoints verified in configuration section - legitimate Skywork domains required for core functionality.

content.user_title: Search the Web for Real-Time Information
content.value_statement: Get current information beyond AI training data by searching the web instantly. Perfect for research, fact-checking, and staying updated with latest developments.
actual_capabilities: Execute up to 3 web search queries in a single invocation
Return structured results with source URLs and content snippets
Handle OAuth authentication with browser-based login flow
Cache authentication tokens locally for reuse
Support environment variable configuration for API endpoints
Save search results to temporary files for later access
limitations: Requires Skywork API authentication before first use
Each query has a 30-second timeout limit
Depends on Skywork search API availability and rate limits

use_cases.title: Research Assistant
use_cases.description: Gather current information and sources for reports, presentations, or decision-making. Search multiple related queries to build comprehensive understanding.

use_cases.title: Fact Verification
use_cases.description: Quickly verify claims, statistics, or recent events by searching authoritative sources. Get source URLs for citation and verification.

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence
use_cases.description: Monitor industry news, competitor announcements, and market trends. Set up searches for specific companies, products, or technologies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup for a single fact or piece of information

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Angle Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search these related queries: 1) [specific aspect 1], 2) [specific aspect 2], 3) [specific aspect 3]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive research requiring multiple perspectives on a topic

prompt_templates.title: Time-Sensitive Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest information about [topic] including any developments from [current year or recent time period]
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting current information that may have changed since training data cutoff

prompt_templates.title: Source-Focused Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [topic] and prioritize results from authoritative sources like [specific domains or publication types]
prompt_templates.scenario: Research requiring high-quality, citable sources for professional or academic work

output_examples.output: Results saved to temporary files containing multiple sources with URLs and content snippets about recent quantum computing developments, including research papers and industry announcements.

output_examples.input: Search these: Tesla Q4 2025 revenue, EV market share 2026
output_examples.output: Two result files created - one with Tesla financial data from investor reports and news, another with EV market analysis from industry research firms, all with source URLs for verification.

best_practices: Use specific, detailed queries rather than broad keywords for better results
Complete authentication before relying on the skill in time-sensitive workflows
Always cite source URLs when presenting search results to users
anti_patterns: Using vague single-word queries that return irrelevant results
Assuming search results are comprehensive - verify with multiple sources
Storing sensitive search queries in persistent files or logs

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Skywork Search?
faq.answer: Run the skywork_auth.py script first. It will open your browser for OAuth login. The token is cached locally for future use. You can also set SKYBOT_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: How many searches can I run at once?
faq.answer: You can run up to 3 queries in a single invocation. The skill processes them sequentially and saves each result to a separate file.

faq.question: Where are search results stored?
faq.answer: Results are saved to temporary files in a system temp directory. The script prints the file paths so you can read them. Clean up temp files after use.

faq.question: What happens if my token expires?
faq.answer: The skill automatically checks token validity. If expired, it initiates a fresh OAuth login flow. Keep your browser accessible for re-authentication.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without internet access?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires internet connectivity to reach the Skywork API endpoints. It cannot function in offline or air-gapped environments.

faq.question: Is my search data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: Yes, your queries are sent to Skywork API servers for processing. Do not include sensitive or confidential information in search queries.
